communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Georgia Power
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of purchase order number 52524873, dated 6/26/2025 from Georgia Power for $6,120.

A copy of all agreements with Georgia Power related to purchase order number 52524873.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Georgia Power
communications.communication:
Good Morning,

The Municipal Clerk’s Office is in receipt of your Open Records Request of Tuesday, May 26, 2026 (see below), in which you requested in part the following information be provided:


                A copy of purchase order number 52524873, dated 6/26/2025 from Georgia Power for $6,120.

A copy of all agreements with Georgia Power related to purchase order number 52524873.



Please be advised that the Municipal Clerk’s Office is not the custodian of the information you are seeking to obtain.  You will need to contact the Department of Procurement via email at openrecordsprocurement@atlantaga.gov<mailto:openrecordsprocurement@atlantaga.gov> or

by telephone at 404-546-1078
Thank you.

Patricia Marshall

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Georgia Power
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act (O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.), the City of Atlanta Department of Procurement acknowledges receipt of your request submitted via email on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at 8:52 AM. Please accept this as the Department’s statutorily required response.


In your request of May 27, 2026, you asked for the following:


A copy of purchase order number 52524873, dated 6/26/2025 from Georgia Power for $6,120.- Attached

A copy of all agreements with Georgia Power related to purchase order number 52524873.- Please be advised that DOP has no responsive agreement or contract document related to your request for Georgia Power. The procurement was processed as a small purchase in accordance with the City of Atlanta Procurement Code. Under the City’s procurement regulations, a small purchase is a procurement made for goods or services that falls within the monetary threshold established for streamlined purchasing procedures and does not require a formal written contract or competitive sealed solicitation process. As such, no formal agreement was executed for this purchase.

Accordingly, there are no additional responsive documents in DOP’s custody and control related to a contract or agreement for this procurement.

Please let us know if you have any additional questions.

[cid:image002.png@01DCEEBB.AACA6C40]

communications.subject: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: Flock ALPR request - Greene County
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the following Indiana license plate numbers:

    140NWB
    552RSB
    224NXB

please provide all Flock Safety / Flock ALPR / automated license plate reader records, detections, “hits,” or plate-read results for the period beginning March 166, 2026 through April 16, 2026.

This request includes, for each responsive record if maintained:

    date and time of detection
    camera or device location
    still image(s) or associated image capture
    plate number as read by the system
    vehicle metadata returned by the system (such as state, make, model, color, body type, or similar fields, if available)
    camera/device identifier
    any exported search results, reports, logs, audit-visible result records, or downloadable output reflecting those detections

If the records are maintained electronically, I request them in electronic form.

This request is for existing records only. I am not requesting that the agency create a new record, perform analysis, or answer questions.

If any portion of this request is denied or withheld, please:

    identify each withheld record or category of record with reasonable particularity,
    state the specific statutory basis for each denial under IC 5-14-3, and
    release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

If the agency contends that responsive records are maintained by a contractor or third-party platform on the agency’s behalf, please treat this as a request for any records the agency owns, controls, can access, or is entitled to obtain through its Flock Safety system or related agreement.

I am willing to pay reasonable copying fees permitted by law up to $100. Please notify me in advance if fees will exceed that amount.

Thank you.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Ratcliff

Muckrock:

Please accept this email as a request for clarification of data request. The Sheriff’s Office considers the request to be a request for “automated hits” and has indicated that records of automated hits are not maintained, only hits on specifically sought inquiries. As such, there is no data on “automated hits” to provide.

communications.subject: RE: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: Flock ALPR request - Greene County
communications.communication:
Mr. Abshire,

The "automated hits" are located in the Flock cloud portal. This portal can be accessed by your Sheriff's Office personnel, and they can search for the responsive records. Is it your position as public records officer that these records do not exist? That is, the Flock cameras do not take images of license plate numbers and use AI to store the plate numbers in a searchable database? Because that's exactly how Flock works.

The automated hits DO exist and they are public records under the Indiana Public Records Act. Other departments, including Monroe, Owen, Lawrence, and Brown, confirm they have this data. Greene county has access as well.

RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Follow-Up FOIL Request: Flock Safety contract authorization, site selection, and cancellation records / Pine Plains
communications.communication:
Dear Jordan,

 

This agency does not maintain or possess the records you have requested.

 

Accordingly, your request is denied. You have 30 days from receipt of a denial of access to records to appeal to: 

 

Name: Brian Walsh             

Title:  Supervisor             

Address: PO Box 955 Pine Plains NY 12567             

Email Address:   <mailto:supervisor@pineplains-ny.gov> supervisor@pineplains-ny.gov  

 

Sincerely,         

 

 

Madelin Dafoe

 

Madelin Dafoe

Town of Pine Plains 

Town Clerk/ RMO/ Notary

PO Box 955 Pine Plains, NY 12567 

Telephone:518-398-7155

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Follow-Up FOIL Request: Flock Safety contract authorization, site selection, and cancellation records
communications.communication:
Dear Supervisor Walsh,

I am appealing the denial of my April 15, 2026 FOIL request, denied by Town Clerk Madelin Dafoe on April 17, 2026, on the ground that the Town of Pine Plains “does not maintain or possess the records” requested.

I respectfully request reversal of that denial.

The basis for my appeal is straightforward. Records previously produced concerning the Pine Plains Flock Safety matter already show responsive communications involving Town officials and offices, including communications involving you and the supervisor’s office. Those records indicate that at least some responsive records were maintained by, sent to, received by, or used by Town personnel in connection with Town business.

My follow-up request specifically sought additional records clarifying contract authorization, signature authority, site selection, relocation of the school-ground site, communications during apparent gaps in the timeline, and records concerning the Town’s later decision not to proceed. In light of the prior production, a conclusory statement that the Town does not maintain or possess responsive records is insufficient without identifying what offices, custodians, accounts, devices, and record systems were actually searched.

Accordingly, I request that the Town:

1. conduct or direct a renewed search of all offices and custodians reasonably likely to possess responsive records, including but not limited to the Supervisor’s Office, Town Clerk, Town Board, Town Attorney, Highway Department, Building Department, and any Town personnel or officials who communicated with Flock Safety or Pine Plains Police Department personnel regarding this matter;

2. search not only email accounts but also attachments, calendars, meeting records, texts, messages, handwritten notes, and records stored on personal devices or non-Town systems if used for Town business;

3. produce all responsive records located; or

4. if the Town continues to deny access, provide a more specific written explanation identifying:

   * the offices and custodians searched,
   * the record systems searched,
   * whether the Pine Plains Police Department was searched or is believed to possess responsive records,
   * whether any records were referred elsewhere internally rather than searched by the Town,
   * and the factual basis for claiming that the Town does not maintain or possess responsive records despite the earlier production.

Please also treat this appeal as a request that any appeal determination be transmitted to the Committee on Open Government as required by FOIL.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this appeal.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter
Independent Investigative Journalist

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: San Rafael Police Department Human Trafficking
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents produced between January 1st, 2023 until March 16th 2026 that include the term "human trafficking" "Eighteenth Street Gang" "Nortenos" "Mafia" "Nuestra Familia" "homeless" "unhoused" produced by the San Rafael police department. 

All police reports pertaining to human trafficking produced between January 1st, 2023 until March 16th 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

title: Draft RCRA permit application for Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF)

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Draft RCRA permit application for Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

The draft Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) permit application submitted for the Savannah River Site Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF). The contractor, Savannah River Nuclear Solutions, told me the draft RCRA permit application was submitted in January.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Salem Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Salem Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

MuckRock News

263 Huntington Ave

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA US Navy ompf 5/11 Sobisky
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Frank Sobisky  
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: Nov 1, 1946
Dates of Service: Approx 1965 to 1973

Request following documents:
1) copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
2) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) Navy commendation medal award citation
4) Presidential Unit citation award citation
5) NAVPERS Form 601-4 record of training/campaigns

Historical Documentation
Redact per 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6)
I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.

Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
A copy of documents responsive to the request.

To Whom It May Concern:

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (South Hadley Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits (South Hadley Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: CIA: MDR: CIA Weekly Summary Special Report, "Moscow and the Georgians," dated 15 December 1972 (Document No. CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CIA: MDR: CIA Weekly Summary Special Report, "Moscow and the Georgians," dated 15 December 1972 (Document No. CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I. Basis for Request
Pursuant to Section 3.5 of Executive Order 13526, I hereby submit this Mandatory Declassification Review (MDR) request concerning the document identified below. Section 3.5(a) of Executive Order 13526 provides that individuals may request a review of classified information for declassification, and that agencies shall conduct a review of such information to determine whether it continues to meet the standards for classification under the Order.

II. Document Identification
Document Title: Weekly Summary Special Report — "Moscow and the Georgians"
Originating Agency: Central Intelligence Agency, Directorate of Intelligence
Document Date: 15 December 1972
Document Number/CREST ID: CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1
Declassification Review Date: Approved for Release 2005/01/11
Publicly Available At: https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP85T00875R001500040043-1.pdf

III. Description of the Document and Nature of This Request
The document at issue is a five-page CIA Directorate of Intelligence Weekly Summary Special Report, classified SECRET at the time of composition, concerning Soviet domestic politics — specifically Moscow's relationship with the Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic and the political downfall of Georgian Communist Party First Secretary Vasily Mzhavanadze following the exposure of widespread economic corruption. The report was approved for partial release in January 2005 but contains multiple redactions citing Exemption 25X1 — the statutory exemption protecting intelligence sources and methods under 50 U.S.C. § 3024(i), as codified through the automatic declassification framework of E.O. 13526. This MDR request challenges the continued application of those 25X1 exemptions on the grounds that the information withheld no longer meets the standards for continued classification under Section 1.1 and Section 3.3 of Executive Order 13526. Specifically, the requestor asserts that:

All named individuals referenced in or near the redacted passages are deceased, eliminating any personal privacy or source-protection rationale for continued withholding;
The underlying historical events — Soviet-era Georgian party politics and corruption investigations of the early 1970s — are thoroughly documented in open academic, journalistic, and governmental literature;
The Soviet state itself ceased to exist on December 25, 1991 — more than three decades ago — eliminating any foreign government sensitivity; and
The passage of over fifty years since the document's creation triggers the presumption of declassification under Section 3.3(a) of E.O. 13526.

The requestor specifically requests a line-by-line review of each redacted passage in the released version of the document.

IV. Deceased Status of All Named Individuals
A core rationale for applying 25X1 exemptions to documents concerning named foreign nationals is the protection of sources who might be identified through context, and who might face retaliation. That rationale is entirely inapplicable here. Every named individual in or proximate to the redacted passages in this document is deceased:

Vasily Mzhavanadze — Georgian SSR First Secretary; died 1988.
Eduard Shevardnadze — Mzhavanadze's successor; later Soviet Foreign Minister and President of Georgia; died July 7, 2014.
Nikolay Tskhakaya — Tbilisi city party secretary; removed from post in May 1972; deceased.
Otari Lolashvili — Georgian MVD chief referenced in the document; deceased.
Leonid Brezhnev — General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union; died November 10, 1982.
Pyotr Shelest — Ukrainian party boss referenced in the document; died January 22, 1996.
Heydar Aliyev — Azerbaijan KGB chief referenced by analogy; died December 12, 2003.
The death of all persons identified in or near redacted passages eliminates any source-protection, retaliation-risk, or personal-privacy rationale for continued withholding under 25X1.

Thank you to the agency and for their commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Flock Safety ALPR Records Request - Riverside County District Attorney

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records Request - Riverside County District Attorney
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following records related to Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems operated by or in partnership with the Riverside County District Attorney's Office:

1. CONTRACTS/AGREEMENTS: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, amendments, renewals, or other legal instruments between the Riverside County District Attorney's Office and Flock Safety, Inc., including pricing, terms of service, and data governance provisions.

2. CAMERA INVENTORY & GPS LOCATIONS: A complete list of all Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed within your jurisdiction, including each camera's physical address and/or GPS coordinates, installation date, and operational status.

3. SEARCH AUDIT LOGS: All audit logs, access logs, or query records showing who accessed the Flock Safety system, when, what license plates were queried, and the stated reason or case number associated with each query.

4. NETWORK/DATA SHARING RECORDS: All records describing which agencies, entities, or networks the Riverside County District Attorney's Office shares Flock Safety data with, including any Hot List sharing agreements, data sharing policies, and agency network participation records.

5. DETECTION/HIT STATISTICS: Aggregate statistics on the number of license plate reads, alerts, hits, or matches generated by Flock Safety ALPR cameras, broken down by time period if available.

6. CAMERA FOOTAGE/READS: All license plate read images, associated metadata (timestamp, location, plate number), and any video or image captures associated with the following individual and vehicle:
   - Name: Anthony Waldheim
   - California License Plate: 9HEE841
   Please search all available records within the system's retention period.

I request records in electronic format. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record or portion thereof.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anthony Waldheim

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R000277-050326
communications.communication:
Dear Anthony Waldheim: 
The Riverside County District Attorney's Office received your request under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) pursuant to Government Code section 7920.000 et seq. on 5/3/2026. Your request will be processed within 10 business days, and you will receive a response via the Public Records Center by 5/13/2026.  Your request is R000277-050326 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting the following records related to Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems operated by or in partnership with the Riverside County District Attorney's Office: 

1. CONTRACTS/AGREEMENTS: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, amendments, renewals, or other legal instruments between the Riverside County District Attorney's Office and Flock Safety, Inc., including pricing, terms of service, and data governance provisions. 

2. CAMERA INVENTORY & GPS LOCATIONS: A complete list of all Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed within your jurisdiction, including each camera's physical address and/or GPS coordinates, installation date, and operational status. 

3. SEARCH AUDIT LOGS: All audit logs, access logs, or query records showing who accessed the Flock Safety system, when, what license plates were queried, and the stated reason or case number associated with each query. 

4. NETWORK/DATA SHARING RECORDS: All records describing which agencies, entities, or networks the Riverside County District Attorney's Office shares Flock Safety data with, including any Hot List sharing agreements, data sharing policies, and agency network participation records. 

5. DETECTION/HIT STATISTICS: Aggregate statistics on the number of license plate reads, alerts, hits, or matches generated by Flock Safety ALPR cameras, broken down by time period if available. 

6. CAMERA FOOTAGE/READS: All license plate read images, associated metadata (timestamp, location, plate number), and any video or image captures associated with the following individual and vehicle: 
- Name: Anthony Waldheim 
- California License Plate: 9HEE841 
Please search all available records within the system's retention period. 

I request records in electronic format. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record or portion thereof. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Anthony Waldheim 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2279423/ 
- 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below by logging into the Public Records Center at the link below. 
 
Thank you, 
Riverside County District Attorney's Office

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Cowboy Concealments
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Cowboy Concealments or Cowboy Streetlight Concealments that were active any time between 1/1/2024 and 5/21/2026.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Cowboy Concealments or Cowboy Streetlight Concealments dated between 1/1/2024 and 5/21/2026. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Worcester Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Worcester Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records maintained by Alma Fuerte Public School for the period January 1, 2023 through the present relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on school property, including work performed with or without a formal contract, and regardless of dollar amount.

Specifically, I am requesting:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

This request seeks copies of existing records only and does not require the creation of new records.
If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please identify the specific exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your response and for providing the purchase order records.

The list provided appears to consist primarily of general procurement and supply purchases from Amazon and do not address the specific categories requested related to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on campus.

To clarify, the request sent on 01/15/2026 is seeking records of:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

If no such records exist for the requested time period of January 1, 2023 through present, please confirm that no responsive records exist.

Otherwise, please provide any responsive records or indicate when they will be made available.

As a follow-up to the original request submitted on January 10, 2026, please provide an update on the status of the request consistent with the timelines set forth under the California Public Records Act.

Thank you for your assistance.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello,

I am writing regarding my Public Records Act request submitted on 01/15/2026, and follow-up on 04/14/2026.

To date, no response has been received addressing the specific categories requested related to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on your campus.

Under the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code §6253(c)), agencies are required to make a determination within 10 calendar days regarding whether responsive records exist and to promptly notify the requester.

The materials previously provided did not address the requested categories, and no further clarification or response has been received following my follow-up.

Accordingly, please:

Confirm whether responsive records related to the request exist, and
Provide any responsive records, or a clear statement that no responsive records exist.

If additional time is required to produce records, please provide a specific date by which responsive records will be made available.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

communications.subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Bennett:

We are in receipt of your request. Please be advised that your earlier
emails were intercepted by the organization's spam filters and did not make
its way to its intended destination. We are now in receipt of your request
and are processing the request accordingly.

We confirm that responsive records exist, however, the nature of the
request appears to be voluminous and broad and is expected to take an
extended time to collect and produce all potentially responsive records as
written. However, we understand from the context and subsequent information
we have received that the request is seeking the below-listed information
specifically in the context of work performed by Done and Done (the
"Subject").

Can you confirm if the request can be considered narrowed as to documents
concerning the Subject?  We make this request pursuant to Government Code
Section 79200.600 "in order to assist the member of the public make a
focused and effective request."  We anticipate that with the scope focused
on the Subject, it would be possible to produce responsive records within
approximately two-three weeks. Any broader scope would be expected to take
a period of months given that it would require a manual search of a
significant volume of records in physical and electronic format by limited
staffing resources (the responding agency is a public school).

This response is without waiver of any privileges or exemptions that may be
claimed as responsive documents are reviewed.

Thank you,
Adriani Leon

-- 

Adriani Leon,
Community Schools Director, Hablo Español!
almafuerteps.org

Alma Fuerte is a public school with a focus on entrepreneurship!
We are a tight-knit and diverse TK-8th community based in Pasadena.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Alma Fuerte,

Thank you for your response.

This Public Records Act request is not intended to be limited to any particular contractor, company, or individual. While I am not familiar with the specific reference to “Done and Done” mentioned in your response, if that is a contractor, vendor, company, or individual/entity that performed construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work on school property during the requested time period, then records relating to that work would be responsive and should be included. However, this request is not limited to only one specific entity.

To clarify, the request seeks records relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on Alma Fuerte school property from January 1, 2023 to present, including:

1. Records identifying each campus construction, renovation, repair, or facilities project, including location and description of work;

2. Records identifying the companies, contractors, vendors, or individuals who performed such work, including associated license numbers, insurance documentation, permits, inspections, approvals, or related compliance documentation;

3. Contracts, agreements, work authorizations, invoices, purchase orders, or procurement records relating to such work;

4. Communications or records reflecting authorization, coordination, approval, or performance of such projects.

The original request was submitted on January 15, 2026. Despite the indication that prior communications were intercepted by a spam filter, the school ultimately did provide a response approximately two months after the original request. However, the records produced consisted primarily of general Amazon purchase records that did not appear responsive to the categories requested above.

Under the California Public Records Act, agencies are required to make a determination within 10 days of receipt of a request and may extend that determination period only as permitted by law. The Act does not permit delay or obstruction of access to public records, nor does it provide an exception allowing a public school to avoid those obligations based on staffing limitations, school size, or the need to search ordinary procurement and facilities records.

The requested records concern recent construction and facilities work and involve basic procurement, contractor, and compliance records that would ordinarily be maintained in the regular course of school operations. Given that the original request was submitted four months ago, and the school has already had substantial time to identify and produce responsive records, an open-ended production timeline extending several additional months is not reasonable.

If certain items require additional time to compile, responsive records should be produced on a rolling basis, beginning with items #1 and #2 above, along with a specific date for the first production and any subsequent rolling productions.

Thank you for your continued attention to the request.

Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Leon,

I am following up regarding my 05/13/2026 message re-clarifying the scope of this Public Records Act request initially made on 01/15/2026 and requesting dates for rolling production of responsive records.

As of today, 05/21/2026, no further response, production timeline, or rolling production date has been received.

Please provide:

1. A date for the first rolling production of responsive records; and
2. An estimated schedule for any subsequent productions.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Steve Bennett

The purpose of this message is to provide you with a notification relating to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). Under NMSA 1978, § 14-2-8, the City has determined that it does not have possession of and/or responsibility for the 911 call audio and transcripts, CAD logs, radio traffic, or any other dispatch records that you have requested.  However, the City knows the proper custodian of these records to be Mesilla Valley Regional Dispatch Authority (MVRDA), 911 Lake Tahoe Ct., Las Cruces, NM, 88007, (575) 526-0795, https://www.mvrda.org/records-request-instructions/.  As such, on December 1, 2025, the City forwarded your request to MVRDA.  To expedite your request, you may consider submitting an additional records request to that agency's records custodian directly.  In light of this notification, the City considers the request for dispatch records closed.  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) 1978, § 14-2-8(C) states, in pertinent part, that a “written request . . .shall identify the records sought with reasonable particularity.” The purpose of this requirement is to provide governmental agencies with the information necessary to accurately and fully respond to public record requests. Upon review, the City has determined that your request, as currently written, is too broad to process effectively. In order to facilitate a thorough and accurate response, the City will separate each case referenced in your request into individual requests. This approach allows us to address each case independently and ensures a more efficient and complete response. 

Accordingly, this request is now closed. Please refer to the respective requests for each case moving forward. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

communications.subject: [Document Released to Requester] MVRDA public records request #25-248
communications.communication:
MVRDA ************************************************************************ Documents have been released for record request #25-248 along with the following message: The Mesilla Valley Regional Dispatch Authority (MVRDA) received your public records request on December 1, 2025 to inspect certain records. After reviewing your request, conducting a search and review of responsive records, we have located: one (1) CAD report and one (1) audio file. A portion of responsive record(s) has been redacted due to protected information exempt that is exempt from disclosure pursuant to: NM Stat §14-2-1.1 for personal identifier information. Responsive records have been released to you. Please see the list below for additional details on no records found for specific incidents/additional information needed: * 9/1/24 near 2125 S ESPINA ST, Dona Ana, NM, (No records found) * 1/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA ST D11, Dona Ana, NM (No records found) * 4/10/25 near E UNIVERSITY AVE/LOCUST, Dona Ana, NM, (Multiple incidents for this day and location. Please provide an approximate time.) * 7/9/25 near 1410 S SOLANO, Dona Ana, NM, (No records found) * 8/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA #11, Dona Ana, NM and (No records found) * 9/5/2025 near EPDC ANNEX, Dona Ana, NM by the Las Cruces Police Department (LCPD), NMSU Police, and DASO. (No records found) MVRDA considers your request complete. * LCPD2025086829CAD_Redacted.pdf * LCPD2025086829.zip Document links are valid for one month. After January 3, you will need to sign in to view the document(s). ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at MVRDA.

Technical support: See our help page

Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at MVRDA.

communications.communication:
Axon - Las Cruces (NM) City Attorney   
    
   
     
 Axon - Las Cruces (NM) City Attorney  
    
 
  
Evidence.com - Evidence Download Link 
 
  
 Misha Gonzales (Badge ID: MG2) from Las Cruces (NM) City Attorney (https://LasCrucesAttorney.evidence.com) has sent you a link to download evidence on Evidence.com. After clicking on the link below you will download 1 zip or iso files containing the requested evidence. This download may take a while depending on your internet connection. 

LCPD 2025-074296, Nine (9) documents in partial response to IPRA P005228-120125. This download link will expire in three (3) days. 
Please note that your access to this link will expire on January 1, 2026 11:10:43 (-07:00). 
Afterwards, you will need to contact the sender to request access. 

Download link: 
[Click here to download] (https://LasCrucesAttorney.evidence.com/axon/download/c8fd681e20754f03824a3b784069676e/547782faa46c4387a7755660cf4a6cb9?token=a9POL23fQaWJlYMadtRxbDr4iK5%2BdlWTiaDKBbsMt%2BvpT2gSgpdDn62cSj437Bsy1D%2Fj3OY9LIZ2e4YFWyPI6mZAPpnaRlX90Z4iwb6nMYJBY2Y40Ei%2F5CwTsGg0D64ozFUcPajTnLeEbznDKpW5z5MFsD9oluexkW0PV3pryAFbvGM87qguVtt2mfDno5bFG7SBtJHIKLv%2BrmAyqf2MEH32YW%2BX%2BXrzO%2B3fWVVAt3w%3D&version=1) 
 
 
 Thank you, 
The Axon Team 
 
 SECURITY NOTICE: Axon will not send you emails that request your username, password, or any other sensitive information. If you receive an email requesting information, it is most likely an attempt to gain access to your account. Furthermore, please verify that any hyperlinks in the message above begin with the proper URL e.g. "https://agencyname.evidence.com". Please forward emails that appear suspicious to help@evidence.com.

The purpose of this message is to provide you with a partial response to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). The City has gathered, reviewed, and appropriately redacted the following public records associated with your request: 

Nine (9) documents 
 
  *  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1.1 states “Protected personal identifier information contained in public records may be redacted by a public body before inspection or copying of a record.” The term “protected personal identifier information” is defined within NMSA Section 14-2-6(F) to include:  (1) all but the last four digits of a: (a) taxpayer identification number;  (b) financial account number; (c) credit or debit card number; or (d) driver’s license number;  (2) all but the year of a person’s date of birth; (3) a social security number; and (4) with regard to a nonelected employee of a public body in the context of the person’s employment, the employee’s nonbusiness home street address, but not the city, state or zip code;  therefore, any of the above-described “protected personal identifier information” found within the responsive records have been redacted. New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1(N) states “[e]very person has a right to inspect public records except as otherwise provided by law.” NMSA Section 31-26-4(A) states that “A victim shall have the right to be treated with fairness and respect for the victim's dignity and privacy throughout the criminal justice process.” The term “Victim” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to mean “an individual against whom a criminal offense is committed. "Victim" also means a family member or a victim's representative when the individual against whom a criminal offense was committed is a minor, is incompetent or is a homicide victim. The term “criminal offense” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to include twenty-one (21) specific crimes. Therefore, any personal information associated within Victim(s) as defined by the Victim’s Rights Act have been redacted. The purpose of this message is to provide you with a partial response to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). The City has gathered, reviewed, and appropriately redacted the following public records associated with your request: 

Two (2) videos 
 
  *  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1.1 states “Protected personal identifier information contained in public records may be redacted by a public body before inspection or copying of a record.” The term “protected personal identifier information” is defined within NMSA Section 14-2-6(F) to include:  (1) all but the last four digits of a: (a) taxpayer identification number;  (b) financial account number; (c) credit or debit card number; or (d) driver’s license number;  (2) all but the year of a person’s date of birth; (3) a social security number; and (4) with regard to a nonelected employee of a public body in the context of the person’s employment, the employee’s nonbusiness home street address, but not the city, state or zip code;  therefore, any of the above-described “protected personal identifier information” found within the responsive records have been redacted. New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1(N) states “[e]very person has a right to inspect public records except as otherwise provided by law.” NMSA Section 31-26-4(A) states that “A victim shall have the right to be treated with fairness and respect for the victim's dignity and privacy throughout the criminal justice process.” The term “Victim” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to mean “an individual against whom a criminal offense is committed. "Victim" also means a family member or a victim's representative when the individual against whom a criminal offense was committed is a minor, is incompetent or is a homicide victim. The term “criminal offense” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to include twenty-one (21) specific crimes. Therefore, any personal information associated within Victim(s) as defined by the Victim’s Rights Act have been redacted. A download link with the contents of this request has been e-mailed to you from AXON. This download link expires in three (3) days. Please contact our office by replying to this email or via telephone if you have any issues accessing the documents. Pursuant to NMSA 1978, § 14-2-10, the City has determined that your written request is excessively burdensome and/or broad. More specifically, the City has determined that it requires an additional reasonable period of time to gather, review, and appropriately redact any and all public records associated with your request. Due to a high volume of requests, the City will be providing rolling disclosures associated with this request. As such, you can expect action regarding this request within thirty-five (35) days. While the City may be able to process a portion of your request before that time, we are not guaranteeing this result. We appreciate your patience and understanding in this matter. 

Christine Rivera, Records Custodian, is the official responsible for any redactions and/or denial of your request. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

The purpose of this message is to provide a status update regarding the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). Due to an unusually large volume of requests, the City is temporarily extending all requests for a period of ninety (90) days. This does not mean that your request will take the full 90-day period. As of the date of this communication, the City currently has a backlog of approximately 1,700 pending IPRA requests, while still receiving approximately 30-40 new requests daily. As you know, it takes time to receive and review each request, document same, gather responsive documents, issue redactions where appropriate, and to disclose the responsive documents to you, the requester. Please note that this 90-day extension policy is temporary in nature, and the City will re-evaluate its position when the backlog returns to sustainable levels, or on July 1, 2026, whichever occurs first. Please note that this is being implemented because the City has received hundreds of requests that pertain to records that are not stored in one location, involving coordination among multiple people, and a significant number of records requests have been received unexpectedly. Normally, the City has adequate staff needs to process its obligations under the Inspection of Public Records Act. However, we are experiencing unexpected exponential growth regarding the number of new requests. The City apologizes for any inconvenience that this may cause, and thanks you for your patience and understanding. 

*If you are an attorney requesting records, please do not utilize the IPRA process to request documents for a matter of pending litigation. The Clerk requests that you instead send a subpoena duces tecum to the City Clerk’s office, copying the City Attorney. Your request will be fulfilled in a more expedited manner. 

Should you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact my office. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Dear Keweenaw County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Keweenaw County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Hi Julie,

Thanks for your April 20, 2026 written response. As I understand your response to be a denial of my FOIA request — since the responsive records are being conditioned on a $1,250 fee not authorized under FOIA — please treat this message as a written appeal under MCL 15.240.

Three things to flag.

1. "Act or statute" in MCL 15.234(10) means a state statute that explicitly authorizes the sale or sets the fee. The Michigan Court of Appeals construed this carve-out in Grebner v. Clinton Charter Twp., 216 Mich. App. 736 (1996):

"A primary definition of the word 'specific' is 'explicit.' … In contrast, the Michigan Election Law, § 522, provides only for the payment of costs of preparing copies of voter registration records, as opposed to their sale. Clearly, this is not explicit authorization of the sale of voter registration rolls."

216 Mich. App. at 743.

MCL 48.101 governs transcripts from the tax roll maintained by the Treasurer's office. It does not explicitly authorize the sale of the Equalization Department's assessment roll, and it does not set a specific fee for such records. The $1,250 figure is a departmental/BoC fee-schedule number, not a fee "specifically provided by an act or statute" within the meaning of MCL 15.234(10).

EAPRA (MCL 15.441–15.446) fails the same test: it doesn't explicitly authorize the sale of assessment rolls, and it doesn't set a specific fee.

2. EAPRA has its own savings clause. MCL 15.443(4): "This act does not limit the right of a person to inspect or copy a public record under [FOIA]." The Senate Fiscal Agency's committee summary for the enacting bill (HB 5832, PA 462 of 1996) puts it plainly:

"The bill would not limit access to a public record under the FOIA. A public record available by enhanced access would have to be made available for inspection or copying in accordance with [FOIA]."

SFA Committee Summary HB 5832 (H-3), 9-18-96, at 2. (Attached.)

3. MI's FOIA has a clear list of exemptions under MCL 15.243. The records I requested are not listed there. FOIA (1976 PA 442) predates EAPRA (1996 PA 462) by ~20 years — if the Legislature had intended EAPRA or MCL 48.101 to make assessment records FOIA-exempt, they would have added them to 15.243.

Side point on custody. I am not requesting anything from the Treasurer's office — no tax billing records, no delinquent tax rolls, no payment receipts, no forfeiture records. I am requesting the assessment roll, which is maintained by the Equalization Department under MCL 211.34 / 211.34d. The assessment roll records valuations (SEV, taxable, classification, PRE). The Treasurer's tax roll records collection. Distinct records, distinct custodians — MCL 48.101 speaks to the Treasurer's records, not Equalization's. I also never requested GIS data.

Requested relief. Pursuant to MCL 15.240(2), I respectfully ask that you:

-- Reverse the denial; and
-- Produce the assessment roll at FOIA cost itemized under MCL 15.234 on the County's cost itemization form.
If this appeal is denied in whole or in part, I reserve all rights under MCL 15.240(1)(b), including a civil action in Keweenaw County Circuit Court with fee-shifting under MCL 15.240(6) and punitive damages under MCL 15.240(7) if the denial is found arbitrary and capricious.

Thanks,
Alexander Munro
awmunro@gmail.com · 734-272-6612
34 N Huron St, OFC
Ypsilanti, MI 48197

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Scott Adams (Dilbert)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Dilbert cartoonist Scott Raymond Adams (June 8, 1957 – January 13, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/13/arts/scott-adams-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Quincy Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions. 
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.] 
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups

Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.
------

I realize this is a large request and appreciate you taking the time to complete it. Please feel free to respond in multiple parts as documents become available.

I have already reviewed: 
A)  Flock Transparency Page: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/saint-louis-park-mn-pd
B) SLP PD ALPR Policy: https://www.stlouisparkmn.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/28047/638681458304130000
C) Location of Devices: https://dps.mn.gov/divisions/bca/data-and-reports/agencies-use-lprs-lpr

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: RE: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

We have received your data request. Given the large scope of this request, a pre-payment of $20.00 at a $0.25 per page fee rate will be required under MN Stat. 13.03 subd. 3(c). We will contact you should additional fees exists after all the reports are gathered or if any payment is due back to you. Please confirm you would like to continue this request or would like to narrow the scope of this request. We will begin responding to your request when pre-payment is received. Please note that after payment is received, because you are not the data subject of this request, the government must respond within a reasonable amount of time.

Please let me know if you would like to move forward with this data request. I will close this data request on March 30, 2026, following Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3(g) if I don’t receive a response.

Data requested:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions.
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.]
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups
Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.


Christine Johnson
